primary_hypotheses.claim: Under identical sizing (EntryVol_12_NoRebal), E0 has higher Sharpe than LATCH
At matched 5% MDD budget using native systems with external cash scaling, LATCH has higher CAGR than E0
primary_hypotheses.effect_size: CAGR(LATCH at 5% MDD) - CAGR(E0 at 5% MDD)

primary_hypotheses.claim: At matched 10% MDD budget using native systems with external cash scaling, LATCH has higher CAGR than E0
primary_hypotheses.effect_size: CAGR(LATCH at 10% MDD) - CAGR(E0 at 10% MDD)

primary_hypotheses.claim: Native LATCH does not have a robust Sharpe advantage over native SM large enough to justify its added complexity

evidence_thresholds.ROBUST: effect direction consistent AND 95% CI excludes 0 AND not highly sensitive to block length
evidence_thresholds.TENTATIVE: effect direction mostly one-sided BUT 95% CI wide or method-sensitive
evidence_thresholds.UNSUPPORTED: 95% CI includes 0 broadly OR effect direction unstable OR strongly method-dependent

requires_both: practical_materiality: Sharpe difference > 0.05 or CAGR difference > 1pp at matched risk
